truth_table: A truth table shows {object_description}. Values use 1 for true and 0 for false. The operators are ! for NOT, & for AND, | for OR, and ^ for XOR.
This truth-table panel shows {object_description}. In the table, 1 means true and 0 means false. Operators: ! NOT, & AND, | OR, ^ XOR.
The Boolean truth table presents {object_description}. Read each row with 1 as true and 0 as false; !, &, |, and ^ denote NOT, AND, OR, and XOR.
A Boolean truth-table display contains {object_description}. Table entries are 0 or 1, with !, &, |, and ^ used for the logical operators.
The panel shows {object_description} in truth-table form. A value of 1 is true, a value of 0 is false, and the headers use !, &, |, and ^.

satisfying_row_count: Evaluate expression P for each row. How many rows make P true?
Using the shown A, B, C rows and expression P, how many rows satisfy P?
What is the number of truth-table rows where expression P evaluates to 1?
For how many input rows is expression P true?
Count all rows satisfying expression P.
truth_pattern_label: Which option gives the output pattern for expression P from top row to bottom row?
Read the rows from top to bottom. Which option matches the truth pattern for P?
Which labeled pattern is produced by expression P across the table rows?
Select the option whose eight values match expression P in row order.
What option shows the correct top-to-bottom output pattern for P?
expression_from_rows_label: Which candidate expression produces the shown P column?
Select the expression label whose truth values match column P.
Which labeled expression matches the completed output column P?
Read column P from top to bottom. Which candidate expression generates it?
Choose the expression option that agrees with every shown P value.

code_prompt_defaults.object_description_satisfying_row_count_: a clean three-variable table with input columns A, B, C and a blank output column headed by expression P
a notebook-style three-variable table with input columns A, B, C and a blank output column headed by expression P
an exam-scan-style three-variable table with input columns A, B, C and a blank output column headed by expression P
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_truth_pattern_label_c: a clean input table, a target expression P, and six labeled output-pattern options
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_truth_pattern_label_n: a notebook-style input table, a target expression P, and six labeled output-pattern options
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_truth_pattern_label_e: an exam-scan-style input table, a target expression P, and six labeled output-pattern options
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_expression_from_rows_: a clean input table with a completed output column P and four labeled expression options
a notebook-style input table with a completed output column P and four labeled expression options
an exam-scan-style input table with a completed output column P and four labeled expression options
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_satisfying_row_count: set "annotation" to an array of pixel-space bounding boxes [x0,y0,x1,y1], one around each full table row that makes expression P true
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_satisfying_row_count: set "answer" to the integer count of rows where expression P evaluates to 1
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_truth_pattern_label: set "annotation" to the pixel-space bounding box [x0,y0,x1,y1] around the selected pattern option card
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_truth_pattern_label: set "answer" to the single capital-letter option label
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_expression_from_rows_lab: set "annotation" to the pixel-space bounding box [x0,y0,x1,y1] around the selected candidate expression card
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_expression_from_rows_label: set "answer" to the single capital-letter candidate label W, X, Y, or Z
code_prompt_defaults.json_output_contract: Use a valid JSON object with keys "annotation" and "answer" in that order for the final answer.
code_prompt_defaults.json_output_contract_answer_only: Use a valid JSON object with key "answer" for the final answer.
